blurb: Apple's network security framework provides layered protections for all data in transit across Wi-Fi, cellular, Bluetooth, and USB connections. The foundation is TLS 1.2/1.3 with App Transport Security enforcing secure connections system-wide, requiring forward-secrecy cipher suites and valid certificate chains by default. Certificate Transparency log checking and OCSP stapling prevent use of misissued certificates. The Network Extension framework enables per-app VPN, content filtering, and DNS proxying in a controlled, sandboxed manner, while allowing enterprise network policies without requiring device-level VPN profiles that inspect all traffic.
